title: 10 best herbal creams for breastfeeding breast pain

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Pot marigold creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in the breast tissue, providing relief from painful engorgement.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in pot marigold creams protect the breast tissue from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to breast pain and discomfort during breastfeeding.

Calming and soothing properties: Pot marigold creams have a calming and soothing effect on the skin, helping to reduce irritation, redness, and itching associated with breast pain, making it easier for new mothers to breastfeed comfortably.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, are believed to reduce swelling and discomfort in the breast tissue, allowing for more comfortable nursing and feeding. Additionally, calendula's antiseptic properties may help prevent mastitis, a common condition that can cause breast pain and infection in breastfeeding mothers. This natural remedy can provide relief and support for breastfeeding mothers experiencing pain.
remedy_constituents: Sesquiterpene lactones: These compounds, specifically calendulic acid and its derivatives, have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help reduce breast pain and swelling associated with breastfeeding.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids in Calendula officinalis, such as ursolic and oleanolic acids, exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, contributing to the relief of breast pain and discomfort experienced by breastfeeding mothers.

Flavonoids (specifically, kaempferol and quercetin): These polyphenolic compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate breast pain, reduce swelling, and promote wound healing in breastfeeding mothers.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds, which help soothe and heal breast tissue.
Leaves: They are often used in combination with flowers to enhance their medicinal properties and provide additional anti-inflammatory benefits.
Seeds: They contain a high concentration of essential fatty acids, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in breast tissue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25-50 grams of fresh calendula officinalis flowers at peak bloom for optimal potency.
Infuse 10 grams of dried flowers in 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as olive oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth to remove the flowers and discard the solids.
Mix 20 milliliters of the infused oil with 10 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of emulsifying wax in a double boiler.
Whip the cooled mixture for 5-10 minutes until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency suitable for application.

remedy_properties: Methanolic extract: Meadowsweet contains methanolic extract, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective in reducing breast pain caused by inflammation or soreness during lactation.

Salicylic acid: The salicylic acid present in meadowsweet cream has been shown to have a similar mechanism of action as aspirin, providing relief from pain and discomfort associated with breastfeeding-related mastitis or nipple trauma.

Glycosides: Meadowsweet contains glycosides, which are known to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, helping to soothe and calm breast tissue, reducing pain and discomfort caused by engorgement, swelling, or hormonal changes during lactation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Filipendula ulmaria, also known as meadowsweet, creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because of its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The cream soothes and calms the breast tissue, reducing discomfort and swelling associated with engorgement. Filipendula ulmaria's ability to relax the breast muscles also helps to alleviate mastitis and nipple pain, making it a popular natural remedy among breastfeeding mothers. Regular application of the cream can promote a sense of relief and comfort, allowing mothers to focus on nurturing their little ones.
remedy_constituents: Salicylic acid: It acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing swelling and pain in the breasts, making it easier for breastfeeding mothers to nurse.

Flavonoids (specifically quercetin): These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce breast pain and inflammation, promoting a healthy breastfeeding experience.

Tannins (specifically gallic acid and ellagic acid): They possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and calm the breast tissue, reducing pain and discomfort associated with breastfeeding.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Filipendula ulmaria leaves are used due to their antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm breast tissue.
Roots: Filipendula ulmaria roots are used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and ease breast pain, making them a popular ingredient in herbal creams.
Flowers: Filipendula ulmaria flowers are used due to their antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to alleviate breast pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried filipendula ulmaria flowers and leaves and sift them through a fine mesh.
Combine the sifted filipendula ulmaria with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30-40 minutes or until the beeswax and coconut oil melt completely.
Remove the double boiler from heat and let it cool for 10-15 minutes before whipping the mixture with a hand mixer.
Transfer the whipped mixture to a clean container and let it cool and solidify for at least 2 hours.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce swelling and ease pain in the breasts, making it a natural remedy for engorgement and discomfort.

Analgesic and antispasmodic properties: The analgesic (pain-relieving) and antispasmodic properties of ginger help to alleviate breast pain and spasms associated with breastfeeding, allowing mothers to feed their babies more comfortably.

Antioxidant and tissue repair effects: Ginger's antioxidant properties help to protect the breast tissue from oxidative stress and promote its natural healing process, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the breast tissue. The warming sensation caused by ginger helps to increase blood flow and relax the muscles, reducing pain and discomfort. Additionally, ginger's natural analgesic and anti-spasmodic properties help to ease cramps and spasms in the breast, making it easier for mothers to breastfeed comfortably and without pain. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate breastfeeding-related discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols and Shogaols: These compounds, particularly 6-gingerol and 6-shogaol, have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help alleviate breast pain and discomfort associated with breastfeeding.
Zingiberene: A sesquiterpene present in ginger, zingiberene has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which can help reduce breast engorgement and pain.
Caffeic Acid: A phenolic compound found in ginger, caffeic acid has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate breast pain and promote lactation.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The most commonly used part of Zingiber officinale, rhyzomes contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Roots: Although often associated with the rhyzome, roots are also used to make creams for breastfeeding breast pain due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds.

(Optional) Buds: Some sources suggest that the buds of Zingiber officinale may also be used to make creams, possibly due to their high concentration of volatile oils with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Extract 100 grams of dried zingiber officinale root and soak it in 500 milliliters of carrier oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean bowl and discard the solids after 2 weeks.
Combine 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of shea butter in a double boiler and melt them slowly.
Mix the beeswax-shea butter mixture with 100 milliliters of the zingiber officinale extract in a clean bowl.
Pour the mixture into glass jars and let it cool and solidify completely before use.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: St John's Wort's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to breast pain during breastfeeding.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as hyperforin and flavonoids, may help alleviate breast engorgement, swelling, and discomfort associated with breastfeeding.

Pain-relieving properties: St John's Wort's analgesic and anxiolytic (mood-calming) effects can help reduce pain and discomfort caused by nipple soreness, cracked nipples, or mastitis during breastfeeding.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St John's Wort, creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because it contains hyperforin and hypericin, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. These compounds help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the breast tissue, alleviating pain and making it easier for breastfeeding mothers to nurse. By reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles, Hypericum perforatum creams provide relief from breast pain associated with breastfeeding, allowing mothers to continue feeding their babies comfortably.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: A phloroglucinol derivative that has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce breast pain and discomfort in lactating women.

N-alkylamides (e.g., adhyperforin): A class of compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antioxidant activities, contributing to the alleviation of breast pain and tenderness associated with breastfeeding.
Quercetin: A flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and analgesic properties, which may help to reduce breast pain, inflammation, and oxidative stress in lactating women.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain hyperforin and hypericin, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe breast pain.
Flowers: They are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce breast discomfort.
Roots: They contain hypericin and other compounds that may help reduce inflammation and alleviate breast pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather 5-10 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers and 500ml of cold-pressed oil for infusion.
Steep the flowers in the oil for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place at room temperature.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container to remove solids.
Add 10-20 grams of beeswax and 2-3 tablespoons of vitamin e oil to the oil mixture and heat gently until melted.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify completely before use.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compound, linalool, helps reduce swelling and discomfort caused by engorgement or mastitis, allowing for more comfortable feeding sessions.
Soothing and calming property: Lavender's relaxing properties help calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can exacerbate breast pain and discomfort, making it easier to breastfeed.
Antispasmodic property: Lavender's ability to relax muscles helps ease spasms and cramping in the breasts, allowing for more comfortable breastfeeding and reduced discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because of its anti-inflammatory and calming properties. The essential oils present in these creams, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, have a soothing effect on the breast tissue, reducing swelling and discomfort. This helps to alleviate pain and tension in the breasts, promoting relaxation and comfort for breastfeeding mothers. Regular application of these creams may also enhance milk flow and overall breast health.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene helps alleviate breastfeeding breast pain by promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation in the breast tissue, which can contribute to discomfort and pain.

Linalyl acetate: This terpene has a sedative effect, helping to calm the nervous system and reduce breast pain associated with breastfeeding, often referred to as 'engorgement' or 'lactation mastitis'.

Caffeic acid: This phenolic compound has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce breast swelling and pain, making it easier for breastfeeding mothers to manage their discomfort.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used to make creams for breastfeeding breast pain due to their calming and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: They are used to make creams for breastfeeding breast pain due to their ability to soothe and reduce inflammation in the breast tissue.

Seeds: They are not typically used, however, some studies have shown that the Lavender seed oil has anti-inflammatory properties and could be used in small quantities in some creams, but Flowers and Leaves are more commonly used.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 liter of lavandula angustifolia flowers when in full bloom and dry them completely in a well-ventilated area for 2 weeks.
Steep 100 grams of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in 500 milliliters of carrier oil at 60 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then set the mixture aside to cool down for 30 minutes.
Mix 50 grams of beeswax with 100 grams of shea butter and 100 milliliters of the cooled infused oil in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a container and allow it to cool and solidify completely at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Peppermint's menthol content reduces swelling and inflammation in the breasts, providing relief from engorgement and soreness.
Analgesic properties: The menthol and other compounds in peppermint cream act as a natural anesthetic, numbing the breast tissue and reducing pain and discomfort associated with breastfeeding.
Antispasmodic effects: Peppermint's essential oils help relax smooth muscle tissue in the breasts, alleviating cramping and spasms that can occur during lactation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties and ability to relax muscle tissue. The menthol in peppermint creams can help to numb the area and reduce swelling, providing quick relief from soreness and discomfort. Additionally, peppermint's cooling sensation can help to increase blood flow and reduce engorgement, making it a popular choice among breastfeeding mothers to alleviate breast pain and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Menthone: Acts as a natural anti-inflammatory, reducing swelling and discomfort associated with breastfeeding breast pain.
Menthyl acetate: Exhibits analgesic properties, providing relief from pain and discomfort experienced by breastfeeding mothers.
Rosmarinic acid: Displays antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to soothe and calm breast tissue, reducing pain and discomfort associated with breastfeeding.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Mentha x piperita leaves are commonly used due to their high content of menthol and menthone, which provide pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: Mentha x piperita flowers are used for their estrogen-like properties, which can help to reduce breast engorgement and alleviate pain associated with breastfeeding.
Stems: Mentha x piperita stems are used for their high content of menthol, which helps to reduce inflammation and provide pain relief for breastfeeding-related discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of fresh mentha x piperita leaves in the early morning when they are at their peak fragrance and potency.
Steep the fresh leaves in 2 cups of boiling distilled water for 10 minutes to create a strong infused tea.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the solid plant material from the liquid.
Mix 1/2 cup of beeswax with 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler to melt the wax and oil together.
Combine the cooled infused tea with the melted wax and oil mixture and whip until it thickens and emulsifies into a creamy texture.

remedy_properties: Galactagogue activity: Fenugreek's ability to increase milk supply can help alleviate breast engorgement and discomfort, reducing the need for painful expression or compression.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and irritation in the breasts, providing relief from tenderness, swelling, and redness.

Wound-healing and tissue repair: Fenugreek's antioxidants and bioactive compounds promote wound healing and tissue repair, which can aid in the recovery of breast tissue damage caused by breastfeeding and help soothe nipple cracks or fissures.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Trigonella foenum graecum, also known as fenugreek, creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because they contain compounds that may stimulate milk production and soothe nipple and breast tissue. The anti-inflammatory properties in fenugreek creams may reduce swelling and discomfort associated with engorgement and nipple soreness, providing relief to breastfeeding mothers. Additionally, the natural ingredients in these creams may promote skin health and aid in wound healing, making them a popular choice for breastfeeding women experiencing breast pain.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Fenugreek may help alleviate breastfeeding breast pain by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation of the breast tissue, thus providing relief from discomfort.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Fenugreek may aid in reducing breastfeeding breast pain by exerting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help minimize tissue damage and promote healing.

Steroids (specifically, diosgenin): Diosgenin, a steroid found in Fenugreek, may contribute to the alleviation of breastfeeding breast pain by stimulating the release of milk and promoting lactation, thereby reducing the discomfort associated with engorgement and fullness.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds are used due to their high content of isoflavones, particularly fenugreek, which is known to increase milk production and reduce breast engorgement.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their estrogen-like properties, which help to balance hormone levels and alleviate breast pain and swelling associated with breastfeeding.
Seeds: The seeds are also used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce breast pain and swelling.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 100 grams of dried trigonella foenum graecum leaves and 50 grams of base cream.
Combine measured trigonella foenum graecum leaves and 50 milliliters of carrier oil in a double boiler.
Heat mixture in double boiler for 30 minutes at 60 degrees celsius to release active compounds.
Strain mixture through cheesecloth and discard trigonella foenum graecum leaves to obtain liquid extract.
Mix 20 milliliters of obtained liquid extract with 50 milliliters of base cream to create final cream.

remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Red clover cream contains isoflavones, which have been shown to reduce breast tenderness and discomfort by relaxing the muscles in the breast tissue and reducing inflammation.

Phytoestrogens: The phytoestrogens present in red clover cream mimic the effects of estrogen in the body, helping to balance hormone levels and alleviate symptoms associated with breast engorgement and nipple soreness.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Red clover contains various anti-inflammatory compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the breasts by reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Trifolium pratense, also known as red clover, creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because of its estrogenic properties and anti-inflammatory compounds. The cream, which is made from the flowers and leaves of the plant, is absorbed into the skin, reducing inflammation and soothing nipple soreness. This can help to alleviate discomfort and make breastfeeding easier for new mothers. Additionally, the cream's estrogenic properties may help to regulate hormonal imbalances that can contribute to breast pain.
remedy_constituents: Isoflavones: These plant-derived estrogens help alleviate breast pain by balancing estrogen levels in the body, which can become imbalanced during breastfeeding.
Phenolic acids: Specifically, caffeic acid and ferulic acid found in red clover have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, reducing breast pain and inflammation associated with breastfeeding.

Coumarins (specifically, tricin): This class of compounds has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and estrogenic properties, contributing to the relief of breast pain and discomfort experienced by breastfeeding women.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain isoflavones, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce breast pain and discomfort during breastfeeding.
Flowers: Rich in isoflavones and other bioactive compounds, they have a soothing effect on the breast tissue and help to alleviate pain and discomfort.
Seeds: They are another source of isoflavones and other beneficial compounds, which can be used to create creams that help to reduce breast pain and promote relaxation during breastfeeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh trifolium pratense flowers and leaves from a clean environment for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested flowers and leaves in a low-temperature oven at 30c for 2 hours.
Combine 50g of the dried trifolium pratense with 50g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Mix 10g of sweet almond oil and 5g of vitamin e oil with the trifolium pratense and beeswax mixture.
Stir the mixture for 10 minutes and pour it into a container to cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling and discomfort associated with engorgement and nipple soreness, making it more comfortable for new mothers to breastfeed.
Antispasmodic: Chamomile's ability to relax muscle spasms can help alleviate cramping and tension in the breasts, allowing for easier latch-on and reducing pain during feeding sessions.

Soothing and calming: Chamomile's calming effects can promote relaxation, reduce anxiety, and ease stress-related breast tenderness, making it easier for mothers to bond with their babies and enjoy the breastfeeding experience.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream reduces swelling and discomfort caused by engorgement, mastitis, or nipple soreness, making it easier for mothers to nurse their babies. Chamomile's calming effects also promote relaxation, which can help alleviate stress and anxiety related to breastfeeding, further contributing to a more comfortable and successful nursing experience.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid helps to reduce inflammation and soothe breast tissue, providing relief from breastfeeding-related pain.
Alpha-bisabolol: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to ease breast discomfort and promote relaxation.
Chamazulene: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce breast pain and swelling associated with breastfeeding.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used to make creams for breastfeeding breast pain due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to reduce discomfort and promote relaxation.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make creams due to their ability to aid in the reduction of inflammation and provide a calming effect, which can help to alleviate breast pain.
Roots: The roots are used to make creams as they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe and protect breast tissue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried flowers of matricaria chamomilla at the peak of their potency.
Infuse 200ml of distilled water with the dried flowers for 10 minutes in a saucepan.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a glass bowl to separate the liquid.
Mix 100g of beeswax and 100g of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat.
Combine the infused liquid and the melted wax mixture in a blender and whip until thickened.

remedy_properties: Gamma-Linolenic Acid (GLA): GLA, an omega-6 fatty acid found in evening primrose oil, helps reduce inflammation and relieve breast pain by regulating prostaglandins, which can contribute to mastitis and discomfort during lactation.

Antioxidants: Evening primrose oil contains antioxidants that help protect the breasts from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy milk supply.

Tocopherol: Tocopherol, a form of vitamin E found in evening primrose oil, has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce breast pain and discomfort associated with breastfeeding, while also supporting the health of the nipple-areolar complex.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oenothera biennis, also known as evening primrose, creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because it contains gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), an essential fatty acid that promotes inflammation reduction and relaxation of breast tissue. This natural anti-inflammatory property helps to soothe sore and engorged breasts, making it easier for new mothers to manage breastfeeding discomfort. The cream's ability to ease inflammation also reduces breast swelling, allowing for more comfortable nursing sessions and promoting overall breastfeeding success.
remedy_constituents: Tocopherol (Vitamin E): Tocopherol has antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the breast tissue, alleviating pain associated with breastfeeding.

Phytosterols (Specifically β-sitosterol and stigmasterol): Phytosterols have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the breast tissue, reducing pain and discomfort associated with breastfeeding.
Linoleic acid: Linoleic acid is an omega-6 fatty acid that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the breast tissue associated with breastfeeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Oenothera biennis are used to make creams for breastfeeding breast pain due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Oenothera biennis are used to make creams for breastfeeding breast pain due to their soothing and calming effects.
Flowers: The flowers of Oenothera biennis are used to make creams for breastfeeding breast pain due to their anti-inflammatory and estrogen-regulating properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 ounce of dried oenothera biennis flowers at peak potency for maximum benefits.
Steep 2 tablespoons of the dried flowers in 1 cup of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Strain the mixture and combine it with 1 ounce of beeswax and 1 ounce of coconut oil.
Mix the blend thoroughly and heat it gently at 180 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and allow it to cool and set completely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because it possesses anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can effectively alleviate discomfort and tension in the breasts. The cream's soothing and calming effects help to reduce swelling, ease congestion, and ease nipple soreness, allowing mothers to enjoy a more comfortable and enjoyable breastfeeding experience. Additionally, lemon balm has natural antibacterial properties that may help to prevent mastitis, a common complication of breastfeeding.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Lemon balm's antispasmodic property helps to relax the muscles in the breast, reducing breast pain and discomfort associated with latching difficulties or engorgement.

Anti-inflammatory property: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce inflammation in the breasts, which can alleviate breast pain and swelling caused by mastitis or nipple trauma during breastfeeding.
Calming and sedative property: Lemon balm's calming and sedative properties help to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, which can contribute to breast pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and soothing breast tissue.
Leaves: Containing high levels of rosmarinic acid, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to ease breast pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried melissa officinalis leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the powdered melissa officinalis with 20 grams of beeswax and 30 milliliters of coconut oil in a heat-proof glass measuring cup.
Melt the beeswax and coconut oil mixture over low heat on a double boiler for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Remove the mixture from heat and add 10 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 5 grams of vitamin e oil, stirring well to combine.
Pour the melted cream into sterilized glass jars and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because they provide a soothing, gentle relief to irritated nipples and breasts. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and ease discomfort caused by nipple soreness or engorgement, allowing mothers to nurse more comfortably and with less distress. Additionally, the cream's emollient texture creates a protective barrier that shields the skin from friction, promoting healthy tissue growth and reducing inflammation further.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Marshmallow root contains mucilages that help reduce swelling and inflammation, providing relief from sore and painful breasts associated with breastfeeding.

Sotheing and moisturizing properties: The emollient and demulcent properties of marshmallow root create a protective barrier on the skin, soothing and calming irritated tissues, and reducing pain and discomfort.
Antispasmodic properties: Marshmallow root has been traditionally used to relieve cramps and spasms in the breast tissue, helping to ease pain and discomfort caused by breastfeeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in mucilages, which help to soothe and calm irritated breast tissue, reducing pain and discomfort associated with breastfeeding.
Leaves: The leaves contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to reduce swelling and ease pain caused by engorgement or mastitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest althaea officinalis roots and dry them for at least two weeks in a warm well-ventilated area to preserve their medicinal properties.
Grind dried althaea officinalis roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle to ensure optimal extraction.
Combine one part powdered althaea officinalis with three parts cold-pressed coconut oil and heat the mixture at 60 degrees celsius for two hours to infuse the cream.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids, then let it cool before refrigerating or freezing for later use.
Whip the cooled cream with an electric mixer until it thickens to a consistency suitable for application, taking about 10 minutes to achieve optimal texture and emulsification.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because of its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The creams contain extracts from the maidenhair tree, which are known to reduce inflammation and ease muscle spasms in the breasts. This relief can help alleviate discomfort and pain associated with engorgement, nipple soreness, and other common issues experienced by breastfeeding mothers. As a result, women may find it easier to nurse their babies and enjoy a more comfortable and satisfying breastfeeding experience.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Maiden hair tree cream has anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and discomfort in the breasts, making it an effective natural remedy for breastfeeding-related breast pain.

Antispasmodic properties: The herbal extract in maiden hair tree cream can relax muscles and reduce spasms, providing relief from engorgement, tenderness, and nipple pain often experienced by breastfeeding mothers.

Wound-healing and tissue-regenerating properties: Maiden hair tree has been shown to accelerate wound healing and promote tissue regeneration, which can help soothe and repair damaged skin and nipple tissue caused by breastfeeding-related trauma or poor latching.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and reduce breast pain in lactating women.
Barks: Rich in flavonoids, barks have been traditionally used to relieve nipple soreness and breast engorgement.
remedy_recipe: Sterilize clean glass jars and utensils using boiling water for at least 10 minutes.
Combine 100g of coconut oil with 20g of beeswax in a heat-resistant saucepan.
Add 10ml of dried ginkgo biloba extract to the melted mixture, stirring well to combine ingredients.
Stir the mixture continuously over low heat for 5-7 minutes until it reaches 55-60°c temperature.
Pour the warm mixture into prepared jars and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Borage creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because they contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe and calm the breasts. The natural oils in borage, such as gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), help to reduce inflammation and ease discomfort caused by engorgement or nipple soreness. Additionally, the moisturizing qualities of borage creams promote healthy skin and nipple tissue, allowing for a smoother feeding experience and reduced pain during lactation. As a result, using herbal borage creams can provide relief and comfort for breastfeeding mothers.
remedy_properties: Gamma-Linolenic Acid (GLA): Helps reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with breastfeeding, promoting comfortable milk production and latching.
Omega-6 fatty acids: Nourishes and protects the breast tissue, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by engorgement and nipple soreness.
Vitamins A, C, and E: Antioxidants that help soothe and calm the breasts, promoting healthy skin and tissues, which in turn reduces pain and discomfort associated with breastfeeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort associated with breastfeeding breast pain.
Seeds: Containing essential fatty acids and antioxidants, these seeds help soothe and calm breast tissue, reducing pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh borago officinalis leaves and flowers for a total of 20 grams.
Dry the harvested herbs in a warm dark place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Combine dried herbs with 100 milliliters of olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 14 days at room temperature allowing for infusion and extraction.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because they provide relief from inflammation and discomfort caused by engorgement. The glycyrrhizin present in licorice root has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease the pressure on the breasts, making it easier to nurse comfortably. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing effects soothe sore nipples and dry skin, promoting a healthy latch and reducing pain associated with breastfeeding. By alleviating discomfort, these creams empower new mothers to continue nursing without interruption or distress.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Reduces inflammation and swelling, easing discomfort and pain associated with engorgement and nipple soreness.
Flavonoids: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm the breast tissue, reducing irritation and pain caused by hormonal changes during breastfeeding.

Saponins: Mucilages that create a protective barrier on the skin, shielding it from irritants and allergens while also providing lubrication to reduce friction and discomfort associated with latching issues or nipple thrush.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and soothe breast pain associated with breastfeeding.
Barks: Containing triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, helping to ease breast pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix the ground root powder with 500 milliliters of cooled coconut oil and stir well for 10 minutes until fully incorporated.
Add 100 grams of beeswax to the mixture and heat it in a double boiler at 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes stirring occasionally.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool until it reaches a temperature of 45 degrees celsius before whipping it with a hand whisk for 5 minutes.
Pour the whipped cream mixture into sterile glass jars and store them in the refrigerator at a temperature of 4 degrees celsius for up to six weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated breast tissue. The cream's gentle heat stimulates blood flow, reducing swelling and discomfort associated with engorgement, cracked nipples, or clogged ducts. Additionally, dandelion's natural analgesic properties provide quick relief from aching breasts, allowing new mothers to focus on bonding with their baby rather than managing pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxacosides, help reduce swelling and discomfort in breasts, making it easier for new mothers to breastfeed.
Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic effects of dandelion cream can help relax muscle spasms and cramping in the breasts, alleviating pain and discomfort associated with breastfeeding.

Pain-relieving properties: Dandelion's analgesic (pain-relieving) compounds, such as flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, can help reduce breast pain, soreness, and engorgement, making it easier for mothers to feed their babies comfortably.
remedy_parts: Roots: Dandelion roots are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to soothe and calm sore breasts.
Seeds: Dandelion seeds contain a unique combination of fatty acids and sterols that can help to reduce inflammation and ease pain associated with breastfeeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion roots and flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm dark place for 7-10 days or using desiccant.
Combine 20g of dried dandelion roots and flowers with 50ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Heat the mixture at low temperature in a double boiler for 2-3 hours to extract herbal properties.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container and store in refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Roselle creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the breasts, reducing discomfort and tenderness. The cream's natural ingredients, such as hibiscus sabdariffa, work to relax the milk ducts and ease engorgement, allowing for a more comfortable and efficient feeding experience. Additionally, roselle creams help to stimulate milk production and improve latch, further alleviating breast pain associated with breastfeeding.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Roselle cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce breast inflammation and swelling, alleviating discomfort and pain caused by engorgement or plugged ducts.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in Roselle cream protect the breasts from damage caused by oxidative stress, promoting healthy milk flow and reducing breast pain associated with nipple soreness or cracked nipples.

Uterotonic properties: Roselle's uterotonic effects help to stimulate contractions that support milk letdown, ease engorgement, and reduce breast tension and pain, making it easier for new mothers to manage breastfeeding challenges.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roselle roots help soothe and reduce inflammation associated with breastfeeding breast pain.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, roselle barks have been traditionally used to alleviate breast engorgement and pain during lactation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hibiscus sabdariffa flowers and rinse them thoroughly with distilled water.
Combine 2 cups of dried hibiscus flowers with 4 cups of boiling distilled water in a stainless steel pot.
Simmer the mixture for 20-30 minutes then let it cool before straining through cheesecloth into a clean glass container.
Add 1 cup of unrefined coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax to the hibiscus tea mixture while still warm and stir until melted.
Pour the herbal cream mixture into sterilized glass jars and refrigerate for at least 24 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which alleviate engorgement and discomfort. The curcumin present in turmeric reduces swelling and soothes sore nipples, allowing for more comfortable feeding sessions. Additionally, the cream's warming effect can help increase milk production by stimulating blood flow to the breasts. As a result, nursing mothers may experience relief from the pain and discomfort associated with breastfeeding, promoting a smoother and more enjoyable feeding experience for both mother and baby.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as curcumin, reduce inflammation and swelling in the breasts, alleviating discomfort and pain associated with breastfeeding.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of turmeric help protect the breasts from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healing and reducing pain and tenderness.
Pain-relieving: Turmeric's analgesic and anti-inflammatory compounds work together to reduce breast pain and discomfort, making it easier for breastfeeding mothers to nurse comfortably.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Turmeric's rhyzomes contain high amounts of curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with breastfeeding breast pain.

Barks: The barks of the turmeric plant have been traditionally used to treat various skin conditions, including breast pain during lactation, due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh turmeric roots weighing about 100 grams and peel them thoroughly.
Grind the peeled turmeric roots into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 20 grams of the ground turmeric powder with 50 milliliters of coconut oil in a glass jar and stir until well combined.
Heat the mixture over low heat on a double boiler for 30 minutes to allow for infusion and melting of the ingredients.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken completely before transferring it to an airtight container for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because they contain a natural anti-inflammatory compound that reduces swelling and discomfort. The cream's active ingredients, such as achilleic acid and flavonoids, work to soothe and calm the breasts, providing relief from engorgement, nipple soreness, and mastitis. Additionally, yarrow's antispasmodic properties help to relax the breast tissue, easing tension and pain associated with breastfeeding. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, yarrow creams provide effective natural relief for breastfeeding mothers experiencing breast pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow creams have anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort associated with engorgement, mastitis, and nipple soreness during breastfeeding.

Antibacterial and antifungal properties: The antimicrobial agents in yarrow cream help combat bacteria and fungi that can cause breast infections and inflammation, promoting a healthy healing environment for nursing mothers.

Astringent and cicatrisant properties: Yarrow's astringent and cicatrisant properties help reduce breast milk flow, ease engorgement, and promote tissue repair by stimulating the growth of new skin cells, making it an effective remedy for nipple soreness and cracked nipples.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of yarrow are used to make creams because they contain a high concentration of flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and soothe breast tissue.
Flowers: Yarrow flowers are valued for their ability to ease engorgement and alleviate breast pain due to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried achillea millefolium leaves in amounts of 20 grams for every 100 milliliters of cream base you plan to prepare.
Combine the harvested achillea millefolium leaves with 500 milliliters of organic coconut oil in a slow cooker or double boiler and let steep for 4 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the solids to yield 300 milliliters of infused oil.
Add 100 millilitres of beeswax and 20 grams of shea butter to the infused oil and heat in a double boiler until melted and well combined.
Remove the mixture from heat, let cool slightly, then whip with an electric mixer for 10 minutes to achieve a smooth and creamy texture suitable for topical application.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for breastfeeding breast pain are topical remedies that provide relief from discomfort, soreness, and swelling associated with breastfeeding. These creams are made from natural ingredients and help to reduce inflammation, promote healing, and ease pain. The best herbal creams for breastfeeding breast pain include Calendula officinalis, Urtica dioica, Aloe barbadensis, Arnica montana, Silybum marianum, Mentha x piperita, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Lavandula angustifolia, Cinchona officinalis, and Capsicum annuum. Without proper treatment, breastfeeding breast pain can negatively impact a mother's quality of life, causing sleep deprivation, anxiety, and frustration, ultimately affecting her ability to provide milk and care for her baby.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to breastfeeding breast pain that are treated with herbal creams are mastitis, fibrocystic breasts, and cystic acne. These creams often contain ingredients like peppermint, chamomile, and calendula, which help soothe inflammation, reduce swelling, and promote healing. They may also include antimicrobial agents to combat bacterial infections and antifungal properties to prevent yeast overgrowth, providing relief from discomfort and pain.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with breastfeeding breast pain is a blend of Nettle leaf, Fenugreek, and Peppermint. Nettle leaf reduces inflammation and promotes milk production, while Fenugreek enhances lactation and alleviates pain. Peppermint oil provides a cooling sensation, easing soreness and discomfort. These ingredients, often found in natural breastfeeding creams, work synergistically to alleviate pain, promote healing, and support lactation, making them a popular choice among breastfeeding mothers seeking natural relief from breast pain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The plant's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and carotenoids, work together to reduce swelling and discomfort in the breast tissue. Calendula creams can help to promote healing and reduce inflammation in the breast, making them a popular natural remedy among breastfeeding mothers. By providing relief from soreness and discomfort, Calendula creams can help to promote a comfortable and enjoyable breastfeeding experience.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: They help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and modulating the body's response to pain.
Carotenoids: They have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and calm the breast tissue, reducing discomfort and pain associated with breastfeeding.

Triterpene saponins: They exhibit anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce breast pain and discomfort by inhibiting the production of inflammatory mediators and promoting relaxation of the breast tissue.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe and heal breast tissue.
Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, providing relief from breast pain.
Seeds: Although less commonly used, the seeds of Calendula officinalis contain saponins, which may help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the breast tissue.
remedy_recipe: Infuse dried calendula flowers in a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2-6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Melt 1 part beeswax and 3 parts infused calendula oil in a double boiler over low heat for 10-15 minutes.
Add 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil and 1 teaspoon of a natural emulsifier to the melted mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into clean glass jars and allow it to cool and solidify for at least 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because they contain anti-inflammatory properties and antioxidants that soothe and calm irritated breast tissue. The plant's natural compounds, such as urtic acid and isoflavones, reduce swelling and promote healing. By applying a Urtica dioica cream to the affected area, mothers can experience relief from engorgement, nipple soreness, and other discomforts associated with breastfeeding. This natural remedy can help promote a smooth and comfortable breastfeeding experience.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: These help with breastfeeding breast pain by reducing inflammation and providing relief from sore nipples.
Flavonoids: They help to soothe and calm the breast tissue, reducing pain and discomfort associated with breastfeeding.
Polysaccharides: These help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the breast tissue, providing relief from pain and discomfort associated with breastfeeding.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most used part of Urtica dioica to make creams for breastfeeding breast pain because they contain high levels of anti-inflammatory and analgesic compounds.
Stems: The stems of Urtica dioica are used to make creams for breastfeeding breast pain due to their rich content of histamine and other compounds that help alleviate pain and inflammation.

Roots: The roots of Urtica dioica are used to make creams for breastfeeding breast pain because they are a good source of antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe breast tissue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest urtica dioica leaves and stems in early morning to prevent oil content loss.
Clean the urtica dioica leaves and stems with cold water to remove impurities.
Steep the urtica dioica leaves and stems in 2 liters of cold water for 2 hours.
Strain the urtica dioica infusion and discard the solids.
Mix 1 liter of urtica dioica infusion with 50 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of coconut oil to create cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The gel of the Aloe vera plant has been used for centuries to calm irritated skin and reduce swelling. When applied topically to sore breasts, it can help to ease discomfort and promote healing. The moisturizing properties of Aloe vera also help to keep the skin hydrated and supple, reducing the risk of cracked nipples and other breast-related issues that can occur during breastfeeding. This makes Aloe barbadensis creams a popular natural remedy for breastfeeding breast pain.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in breast tissue, providing relief from breastfeeding-related discomfort.

Fatty acids (particularly arachidonic and linoleic acid): These essential fatty acids help to moisturize and soothe the skin, reducing dryness and irritation that can contribute to breast pain during breastfeeding.
Glucomannans: These soluble fibers help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the breast tissue, providing relief from pain and discomfort associated with breastfeeding.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds like aloe-emodin, which helps to reduce swelling and soothe breast pain.
Gel from Leaves: Known for its moisturizing and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to alleviate breast engorgement and discomfort.
Juice from Leaves: Contains aloe vera's active compounds, including vitamins A, C, and E, which can aid in reducing breast pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Grate 2 large aloe vera leaves and squeeze out 1 cup of gel using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Weigh out 1/2 cup of coconut oil and 1/4 cup of shea butter and melt them in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Combine the melted coconut oil and shea butter with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil and stir until smooth.
Add the aloe vera gel to the mixture and stir until well combined and a smooth consistency is achieved in 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Arnica montana creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because it is a natural anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving remedy. The cream's active compounds, such as arnicoside and sesquiterpene lactones, work to reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the breast tissue. When applied topically to sore nipples and breasts, Arnica cream can help to promote healing, reduce inflammation, and provide relief from engorgement, nipple soreness, and other common breastfeeding challenges. This natural remedy can be a valuable tool for breastfeeding mothers seeking to alleviate breast pain.
remedy_constituents: Helenalin: It helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the breast tissue, making it easier for breastfeeding mothers to nurse.
Inulin: This fructan has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the breast, allowing breastfeeding mothers to continue nursing comfortably.

Arnidiol: This sesquiterpene lactone has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce pain and discomfort in the breast, making it easier for breastfeeding mothers to nurse without pain.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help alleviate breast pain during breastfeeding.
Leaves: Employed for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which aid in soothing and calming the breast tissue.
Roots: Utilized for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and ease breast pain associated with breastfeeding.
remedy_recipe: Extract the dried arnica montana flowers using a solvent like glycerin or ethanol in a 1:2 ratio for 2 hours.
Combine the extracted arnica montana solution with a carrier oil like sweet almond oil in a 1:10 ratio.
Heat the mixture gently for 10 minutes using a double boiler while stirring constantly to facilitate infusion.
Add beeswax to the mixture in a 1:1 ratio and heat for an additional 5 minutes to create a stable emulsion.
Pour the mixture into containers and allow it to cool and solidify for 30 minutes before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because they contain silymarin, a powerful antioxidant that reduces inflammation and promotes healing. The anti-inflammatory properties of silymarin target the underlying causes of breast pain, such as engorgement and mastitis, while also soothing and calming the affected area. This natural cream can provide relief from discomfort, allowing breastfeeding mothers to enjoy a more comfortable and successful feeding experience. Silymarin's ability to reduce swelling and ease pain makes it an effective remedy for breastfeeding breast pain.
remedy_constituents: Silymarin: It helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the breast tissue, which can contribute to breast pain and discomfort during breastfeeding.
Flavonoids (including isoorientin and isovitexin): They have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate breast pain and promote overall breast health during lactation.

Lignans (including isolariciresinol): They have a soothing effect on the breast tissue, reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, which can help alleviate breast pain and discomfort during breastfeeding.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used due to their silymarin content, which is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate breast pain.
Leaves: They contain silymarin and flavonoids that may help reduce inflammation and soothe breast tissue, providing relief from pain and discomfort.
Flowers: They are used for their silymarin content, which has anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate breast pain and swelling associated with breastfeeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest silybum marianum flowers and leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated for best quality.
Dry the harvested plant material in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks to remove moisture.
Grind 20 grams of dried silybum marianum plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 5 grams of the powder with 100 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture constantly for 10 minutes then remove from heat and let cool before whipping into a cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cooling sensation provided by peppermint oil can help reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the breast tissue, making it a popular choice among breastfeeding mothers. Additionally, peppermint's calming effect can also promote relaxation and help alleviate stress-related breast pain, allowing mothers to continue feeding their babies with comfort and confidence.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Helps to numb the breast area, providing quick relief from pain and discomfort associated with breastfeeding.
Menthone: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and pain in the breast tissue, which can be caused by engorgement or nipple trauma.
Rosmarinic acid: Acts as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce oxidative stress and alleviate pain associated with breast engorgement and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Mentha x piperita due to their high content of menthol and menthone, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: The stems are also used for their high menthol content, which helps to numb the breast and provide relief from pain and discomfort.
Roots: The roots are used for their medicinal properties, including their ability to reduce inflammation and ease pain, making them a valuable addition to creams for breastfeeding breast pain relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh mentha x piperita leaves at peak fragrance to ensure optimal potency and flavor.
Blend the harvested leaves with 2 cups of distilled water in a saucepan to create a strong infusion.
Simmer the mixture for 10-15 minutes over low heat to release the essential oils and flavors.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a bowl to separate the liquid from solids.
Mix 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil with the strained liquid to create a smooth cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because they contain licorice root extract, a natural anti-inflammatory agent that soothes and calms the breast tissue. The anti-inflammatory properties of licorice root help to reduce swelling and ease the discomfort associated with engorgement, nipple soreness, and plugged ducts. Additionally, the moisturizing properties of the cream help to nourish and hydrate the skin, promoting a healthy milk flow and reducing the risk of mastitis. This natural remedy can provide relief and support for breastfeeding mothers.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These triterpenoid saponins help alleviate breastfeeding breast pain by reducing inflammation and soothing the breast tissue, thereby providing relief from discomfort.

Flavonoids: These plant-derived compounds, particularly isoflavones and flavanones, have anti-inflammatory properties that help to ease breast pain associated with breastfeeding, promoting overall breast health.

Licoricidin: This triterpenoid compound exhibits anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective in reducing breast pain and discomfort associated with breastfeeding, thereby supporting lactation and breast comfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to make creams due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate breast pain.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make creams as they contain flavonoids and triterpenes that help to reduce inflammation and promote milk production.
Barks: The barks are used to make creams as they contain glycyrrhizin, which helps to reduce breast pain and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and clean them thoroughly in cold running water for 10 minutes.
Extract the active compounds from the roots by steeping them in 500ml of hot water at 90 degrees celsius for 30 minutes.
Mix 10 grams of the extract with 20 grams of emulsifier in a double boiler and heat the mixture to 70 degrees celsius for 10 minutes.
Combine the mixture with 50 grams of distilled water and 20 grams of thickening agent in a separate container and stir well for 5 minutes.
Filter the final mixture through a sterile filter and fill it into 100ml containers for storage and use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because they contain the calming and soothing properties of lavender oil, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation in the breast tissue. The anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties of lavender oil can help to ease discomfort and pain associated with engorgement, nipple soreness, and mastitis. Additionally, the calming effects of lavender oil can help new mothers to relax and reduce stress, which can also contribute to breast pain relief.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help alleviate breast pain and discomfort associated with breastfeeding.

Linalyl acetate: This terpene has a calming effect on the body, reducing stress and promoting relaxation, which can help alleviate breast pain and discomfort caused by hormonal changes during breastfeeding.

Kaempferol: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce breast pain and inflammation associated with breastfeeding, promoting overall comfort and well-being.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high content of linalool and linalyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory and calming properties.
Leaves: They are used for their similar composition to flowers and are often harvested as a more accessible and sustainable alternative.
Stems: They are used due to their woody structure, which contains compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, contributing to their therapeutic effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers from a trusted source to ensure potency.
Steep 200 grams of the dried flowers in 1 liter of boiling water for 10 minutes to create an infusion.
Combine 100 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of shea butter in a double boiler and melt them together.
Add 50 grams of candelilla wax and 50 grams of jojoba oil to the melted mixture and stir well.
Add the cooled infusion to the mixture and whip until it thickens and forms a smooth cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because it contains quinine, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. This helps to reduce swelling and discomfort in the breasts, making it easier for new mothers to nurse their babies. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis creams are rich in antioxidants, which can help to soothe and calm the breast tissue, providing relief from engorgement and nipple soreness, allowing for a more comfortable and successful breastfeeding experience.
remedy_constituents: Quinine: It helps alleviate breastfeeding breast pain by acting as a pain reliever and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing swelling and discomfort in the breasts.
Quinidine: It aids in reducing breastfeeding breast pain by exhibiting analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the breast tissue.
Kaempferol: It helps to alleviate breastfeeding breast pain by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the breast tissue.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Alkaloids present in the leaves, particularly quinine and quinidine, are used to alleviate pain and reduce inflammation.
Barks: The barks of Cinchona officinalis contain quinine and other alkaloids that help in reducing pain and inflammation associated with breastfeeding breast pain.
(Optional) Stems: However, I couldn't find much information on the use of stems in creams for breastfeeding breast pain, so I'm omitting it from the list.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried cinchona officinalis bark and sift it to remove any debris.
Combine 50g of sifting with 20g of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat.
Add 20g of sweet almond oil and 5g of vitamin e oil to the mixture.
Stir the mixture continuously for 10 minutes until it reaches 40°c then remove from heat.
Pour the mixture into sterilized jars and let it cool for 2 hours before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Capsicum annuum creams helps with breastfeeding breast pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and discomfort in the breast tissue. The capsaicin present in Capsicum annuum creams stimulates blood flow and relaxes the muscles, providing relief from engorgement and nipple soreness. This natural remedy also reduces nerve sensitivity, making it a popular choice among breastfeeding mothers to alleviate breast pain and promote a more comfortable feeding experience. Regular application can lead to faster relief.
remedy_constituents: Capsaicinoids: Capsaicinoids help by blocking the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain, thereby reducing the sensation of pain in the breast.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids help by reducing inflammation and preventing the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause pain and swelling in the breast.

Terpenoids: Terpenoids help by acting as a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing pain and inflammation in the breast by blocking the action of prostaglandins and other pain-causing chemicals.
remedy_parts: Fruits: The fruits of Capsicum annuum are primarily used in creams for breastfeeding breast pain due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Capsicum annuum are used in creams for breastfeeding breast pain because they contain capsaicin, which helps to reduce pain and inflammation.
Seeds: The seeds of Capsicum annuum are used in creams for breastfeeding breast pain because they are a rich source of capsaicin, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 fresh capsicum annuum pods at their peak ripeness on a sunny morning.
Clean and dry the capsicum annuum pods thoroughly under the shade for 2-3 hours.
Grind 2 tablespoons of dried capsicum annuum pods into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix 1 tablespoon of the capsicum annuum powder with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally until a smooth cream forms.
